Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails (query: "PivotPoint Labs AND (discount returning customer OR 30% OR website OR invoice")). Find the complaint email sent to Catalina Dubois from Sam at PivotPoint Labs stating that the MoveOps website advertises a 30% discount for returning customers, that PivotPoint received no discount whatsoever, and that their invoice reflects the full price.
OE 2: Reply to the complaint using reply_to_email (email_id: 28e1c274ef90416aab8c5b08fc339072, sender: "catalina.dubois@moveops.com", content: "Acknowledgment of the email and that it's being investigated"). Send a brief acknowledgement telling PivotPoint Labs that MoveOps is looking into the discount issue internally.
OE 3: Search CRM for the client company using crm_search_companies (name: "PivotPoint Labs"). Find the CRM company record for PivotPoint Labs, confirming it is an existing client account.
OE 4: Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_pivot"). Find any active or in-progress deal for PivotPoint Labs.
OE 5: Update in-progress deal using crm_update_deal_stage (id: "deal_pivot_april_2026_dual", dealstage: "at_risk"). Mark the active deal as at risk because PivotPoint Labs has raised a pricing complaint.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks customers using quickbooks_search_customers (criteria: DisplayName LIKE PivotPoint Labs). Find the QuickBooks customer record for PivotPoint Labs.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: CustomerRef LIKE PivotPoint Labs). Find any invoices, estimates, or pricing already sent to PivotPoint Labs related to the current complaint.
OE 8: Read the relevant invoice using quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: "inv_pivot_kovac"). Confirm this invoice amount has already been sent so it can be included in the complaint ticket.
OE 9: Read the relevant invoice using quickbooks_read_invoice (invoice_id: "inv_pivotpoint_april_2026_dual"). Confirm this invoice amount ($10,650) has already been sent so it can be included in the complaint ticket.
OE 10: Create a Linear complaint ticket using linear_create_issue (team: "Customer Engagement", title: "PivotPoint Labs complaint - 30% returning-customer discount not applied", description including the complaint details and any invoice amounts or pricing already sent). The ticket should track the complaint for Catalina Dubois and David Chen.
OE 11: Log the complaint in the CRM using crm_create_engagement(engagement_type: "complaint", body: "Customer complaint: PivotPoint Labs did not receive advertised 30% returning customer discount.", title: "PivotPoint Labs - Missing Discount Complaint", company_ids: ["company_pivot"], description: "Customer complained about not receiving the 30% discount for returning customers. Internal investigation is underway.")
OE 12: Send David Chen an email using send_email (sender: catalina.dubois@moveops.com, recipients: ["david.chen@moveops.com"]). Tell him that PivotPoint Labs has raised a complaint about the 30% returning-customer discount and ask him to check the complaint ticket.
OE 13: Search emails using search_emails (query: from:elena.rostova@moveops.com to:priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com discount OR "30%" OR website OR Discounts). Find Elena Rostova's email to Priya Chakrabarti stating that returning customers are being shown on the website as qualifying for a 30% discount, and instructing Priya to create a Slack channel called Discounts and post an announcement so the team applies the 30% discount going forward.
OE 14: Read Elena's email using get_email_by_id (email_id: "183bcbe012ff4b7b8ce258a64f1e6032"). Confirm that Priya was explicitly told to: (a) create a Slack channel called Discounts, and (b) post an announcement there informing the team that they need to apply the 30% discount to all returning customers going forward.
OE 15: List Slack channels using channels_list ( channel_types: "public_channel,private_channel,im,mpim"). Confirm that no Slack channel named Discounts exists, meaning Priya did not complete Elena's specific instruction.
OE 16: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Discounts OR discount OR 30% OR returning customers"). Find no evidence that Priya posted an announcement informing the team about the new 30% returning-customer discount, or find only scattered references that do not amount to a proper rollout.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 An email was sent (via reply_to_email or send_email) from [catalina.dubois@moveops.com](mailto:catalina.dubois@moveops.com) to [sam@pivotpointlabs.com](mailto:sam@pivotpointlabs.com).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a successful send_email call with sender catalina.dubois@moveops.com and recipient sam@pivotpointlabs.com, returning email_id 6c1bf6e9459f41e7b31f275784402f41.
✓Run #2The agent first attempted reply_to_email which failed (email not found), then successfully used send_email with sender catalina.dubois@moveops.com and recipient sam@pivotpointlabs.com (email_id: 39d533d705e949b7b5be4ec06c2bbc1a).
✓Run #3The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'pivotpoint_dual_reloc_followup' (an email from sam@pivotpointlabs.com) and sender 'catalina.dubois@moveops.com'. The reply went to Sam at PivotPoint Labs. The call succeeded and returned a new email_id.
✓Run #4A send_email call was made with sender catalina.dubois@moveops.com and recipient sam@pivotpointlabs.com, which succeeded and returned email_id 52e983e2d22241f897096cf2ef4e5d30.
✓Run #5The agent first attempted reply_to_email which failed (email not found), then successfully called send_email from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to sam@pivotpointlabs.com, which returned a successful email_id.
✓Run #6The agent called reply_to_email with sender 'catalina.dubois@moveops.com' on the PivotPoint dual relocation thread ('pivotpoint_dual_reloc_followup'). The call succeeded (returned email_id '430248902c034dc88f2f8a31f4348dd3'), meaning an email was sent from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to the PivotPoint Labs thread, which goes to sam@pivotpointlabs.com.
2 The email body (in the reply_to_email or send_email content parameter) to [sam@pivotpointlabs.com](mailto:sam@pivotpointlabs.com) states that the discount issue is being investigated or similar.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content says 'I'm looking into the repeat-customer discount question right now' and 'we're reviewing the pricing across all of your invoices to make sure everything is correct', clearly stating the issue is being investigated.
✓Run #2The email body to sam@pivotpointlabs.com states: 'I'm personally looking into this right now — I want to understand exactly what happened with the discount and ensure we get to the bottom of it.' This clearly communicates that the issue is being investigated.
✓Run #3The reply content explicitly states 'I've flagged your concern about the repeat-customer discount internally and am working with our team to review the pricing on your account' and 'are looking into it right away,' clearly communicating investigation of the discount issue.
✓Run #4The email content explicitly states 'I'm personally looking into this right now — reviewing your account history, our pricing records, and the website language you referenced,' which clearly communicates that the discount issue is being investigated.
✓Run #5The send_email content to sam@pivotpointlabs.com explicitly states 'I'm personally looking into the discount concern right now' and 'I've escalated this internally so our Customer Engagement Lead, David Chen, and I can review it together,' clearly communicating the issue is being investigated.
✓Run #6The reply content explicitly states 'I'm personally looking into this right now — reviewing our pricing records, your invoices, and the information on our website to understand exactly what happened.' This clearly communicates investigation is underway.
3 The PivotPoint Labs deal deal_pivot_april_2026_dual was updated (via crm_update_deal_stage) to at_risk.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent attempted crm_update_deal_stage with dealstage 'atrisk' but it FAILED with an error stating 'atrisk' is not a valid stage. The agent then set the deal to 'qualifiedtobuy' instead, which is not 'at_risk'. The deal was never successfully updated to at_risk.
✗Run #2The agent attempted crm_update_deal_stage with id=deal_pivot_april_2026_dual and dealstage=at_risk, but the call failed with error 'Invalid deal stage: at_risk. Valid stages: [appointmentscheduled, qualifiedtobuy, ...]'. The deal was never actually updated to at_risk.
✗Run #3The agent called crm_update_deal_stage with id='deal_pivot_april_2026_dual' and dealstage='atrisk', but the tool returned an error: 'Invalid deal stage: atrisk. Valid stages: [appointmentscheduled, qualifiedtobuy, ...]'. The agent never retried with a valid stage, so the deal was never actually flagged as at_risk.
✗Run #4The agent attempted crm_update_deal_stage with dealstage 'atrisk' which failed (invalid stage). It then used 'decisionmakerboughtin' instead of 'at_risk', which is not the required stage value. The deal was never properly set to at_risk.
✗Run #5The agent attempted crm_update_deal_stage with dealstage 'atrisk' which failed (invalid stage). It then called it again with 'decisionmakerboughtin' as a workaround, which is not 'at_risk'. The deal was never properly flagged as at_risk.
✗Run #6The agent called crm_update_deal_stage with id='deal_pivot_april_2026_dual' and dealstage='atRisk', but the tool returned an error: 'Invalid deal stage: atRisk. Valid stages: [appointmentscheduled, qualifiedtobuy, ...]'. The agent did not retry with 'at_risk' or any other valid format. The deal was never successfully updated.
4 A Linear complaint ticket was created (via linear_create_issue) in team Customer Engagement with a title related to PivotPoint Labs complaint - 30% returning-customer discount not applied.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The linear_create_issue call used team 'Operations' (team_operations), not 'Customer Engagement'. The criterion specifically requires the ticket be in the Customer Engagement team.
✗Run #2The linear_create_issue call used team='Operations' (resulting in team_id='team_operations'), not 'Customer Engagement'. The ticket was successfully created but in the wrong team.
✗Run #3The agent created a Linear issue but used team 'Operations' (team_id: team_operations), not 'Customer Engagement'. The linear_list_teams call confirmed no 'Customer Engagement' team exists, but the criterion specifies that team. The ticket was created in the wrong team.
✗Run #4The linear_create_issue call used team 'Operations' (team_id: team_operations), not 'Customer Engagement' as required by the criterion.
✗Run #5The agent created a Linear issue with team 'Operations', not 'Customer Engagement' as required. The linear_create_issue call used team='Operations'.
✗Run #6The agent created a Linear issue, but used team='Operations' (team_id: team_operations), not 'Customer Engagement'. The criterion requires the ticket be in the Customer Engagement team.
5 The complaint ticket description (in the linear_create_issue description parameter) includes invoice amounts or pricing already sent to PivotPoint Labs, such as $5,803 and $10,650
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Linear ticket description includes a full invoice table with $5,803 (Kovac), $5,000 (Greenwald), $10,650 (dual relocation), and a $21,453 total — exactly the invoice amounts sent to PivotPoint Labs.
✓Run #2The Linear ticket description includes detailed invoice amounts: $5,803 (Kovac), $5,000 (Greenwald), $10,650 (Okonjo + Castañeda dual relocation), and a total of $21,453.
✓Run #3The Linear ticket description explicitly includes invoice amounts: $5,803 (Invoice NMX-2026-0034 for Ines Kovac), $5,000 (Invoice INV-2026-0304 for Sasha Greenwald), and $10,650 (Invoice INV-2026-0315 for Okonjo + Castañeda). Both required dollar amounts are present.
✓Run #4The Linear ticket description includes invoice amounts $5,803 (NMX-2026-0034, Ines Kovac) and $10,650 (INV-2026-0315, Okonjo + Castañeda), as well as $5,000 (INV-2026-0304, Sasha Greenwald).
✓Run #5The Linear ticket description explicitly includes invoice amounts: NMX-2026-0034 ($5,803), INV-2026-0304 ($5,000), and INV-2026-0315 ($10,650), meeting the requirement to include invoice amounts/pricing already sent.
✓Run #6The linear_create_issue description field includes Invoice NMX-2026-0034 at $5,803, INV-2026-0304 at $5,000, and INV-2026-0315 at $10,650, along with itemized breakdowns. Invoice amounts were clearly included.
6 A CRM engagement was created (via crm_create_engagement) for company ID company_pivot with a title related to PivotPoint Labs - Missing Discount Complaint.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2crm_create_engagement was successfully called with company_ids: ['company_pivot'] and a title related to 'COMPLAINT: Returning Customer Discount Not Applied — PivotPoint Labs'.

✓Run #4A crm_create_engagement call was made with company_ids containing 'company_pivot', successfully logging a complaint engagement on PivotPoint Labs' record.

7 The CRM engagement body or description (in the crm_create_engagement body or description parameter) states that PivotPoint Labs complained about not receiving the advertised 30% returning-customer discount and that an internal investigation is underway or similar.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The CRM engagement body states 'Sam Delgado contacted us stating that PivotPoint Labs was not given a repeat-customer discount that he believes is advertised on the MoveOps website' and lists 'Actions taken' including investigation steps. Investigation is clearly implied.
✓Run #2The CRM engagement body states: 'Sam Delgado (CEO) filed a formal complaint stating that the MoveOps website advertises a 30% returning customer discount...Resolution pending investigation into whether the website actually advertises this discount.' Both the complaint and investigation are documented.
✓Run #3The CRM engagement body states 'PivotPoint Labs (Sam Delgado, CEO) has filed a complaint that they were not offered a repeat-customer discount that they believe is advertised on the MoveOps website' and notes 'Linear ticket created for tracking. outcome

✗Run #1While the agent correctly states PivotPoint was billed at full/standard rates, it did not confirm that the website actually advertises a 30% returning-customer discount. The response says 'whether or not the website explicitly says repeat-customer discount,' indicating the agent never found the 30% website claim — a key part of the criterion.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly states: 'All three PivotPoint invoices were issued at full standard pricing with zero discounts: $5,803 (Kovac), $5,000 (Greenwald), $10,650 (Okonjo + Castañeda).' The complaint context about the 30% website discount is also clearly documented.
✗Run #3The final response does not mention that PivotPoint Labs was billed at full price or that the website advertises a 30% returning-customer discount. The short final response lacks any substantive root cause summary.
✗Run #4While the response does confirm PivotPoint was billed at standard/full rates, it never confirms that the website advertises a 30% returning-customer discount. The summary says 'We couldn't find any internal discussion of specific website copy' and treats the website discount claim as unverified.
✗Run #5The final response concludes that no discount program exists ('MoveOps does not have a 30% returning customer discount program') rather than finding that the website does advertise a 30% discount but it wasn't applied. The agent failed to identify the actual situation where the website advertised a discount that wasn't honored.
✗Run #6The final response says no discount was applied to PivotPoint Labs' invoices, but frames the 30% claim as likely false ('no evidence that a 30% returning customer discount is a real MoveOps policy'). It does not state that the website actually advertises such a discount while PivotPoint was billed at full price — it treats the discount claim as potentially invalid rather than confirmed real.
